SEARCH
Package model

Class SortStats

java.lang.Object
model.SortStats
public class SortStats extends Object
Keeps track of stats for a sorting algorithm while it runs. Stores things like how many comparisons and swaps happened, and also records them over time so we can plot them later. Was updated to also hold benchmark metadata like list size, entropy, and run number for more detailed analysis.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    SortStats(String algorithmName, String generatorLabel)
    Creates a SortStats with just an algorithm name and input label.
    SortStats(String algorithmName, String generatorLabel, String generatorType, int size, double entropy, int run)
    Creates a SortStats with full benchmark metadata attached.

  • Constructor Details

    • SortStats

      public SortStats(String algorithmName, String generatorLabel)
      Creates a SortStats with just an algorithm name and input label. The benchmark fields are set to default placeholder values since we don't have that info here. Used by the older parts of the code.
      Parameters:
      algorithmName - the name of the sorting algorithm
      generatorLabel - describes the kind of input used

    • SortStats

      public SortStats(String algorithmName, String generatorLabel, String generatorType, int size, double entropy, int run)
      Creates a SortStats with full benchmark metadata attached. Use this one when running proper benchmarks where you want to track results across different sizes, entropies, and multiple runs.
      Parameters:
      algorithmName - the name of the sorting algorithm
      generatorLabel - describes the kind of input used
      generatorType - the type of generator used (e.g. "SwapSort")
      size - the size of the list that was sorted
      entropy - the entropy level of the input (0.0 = sorted, 1.0 = fully random)
      run - which run number this is in the benchmark

  • Method Details

    • start

      public void start()
      Resets everything and starts the timer. Should be called right before the sort begins.

    • incrementComparisons

      public void incrementComparisons()
      Call this every time the algorithm does a comparison. It increments the counter and saves the current time and total so we can graph how comparisons grew over time.

    • incrementSwaps

      public void incrementSwaps()
      Call this every time the algorithm does a swap. Same idea as incrementComparisons, it saves the timestamp too.

    • incrementAccesses

      public void incrementAccesses()
      Call this every time the algorithm reads or writes to the array. It just increments the counter.

    • getComparisonTimes

      public List<Long> getComparisonTimes()
      Returns the list of timestamps (in ms) at which each comparison happened. Used together with getComparisonValues() to draw a graph.
      Returns:
      list of timestamps for each comparison

    • getComparisonValues

      public List<Long> getComparisonValues()
      Returns the running total of comparisons at each recorded point in time.
      Returns:
      list of cumulative comparison counts

    • getSwapTimes

      public List<Long> getSwapTimes()
      Returns the list of timestamps (in ms) at which each swap happened.
      Returns:
      list of timestamps for each swap

    • getSwapValues

      public List<Long> getSwapValues()
      Returns the running total of swaps at each recorded point in time.
      Returns:
      list of cumulative swap counts

    • stop

      public void stop()
      Stops the timer. It should be called right after the sort finishes.

    • getComparisons

      public long getComparisons()
      Returns:
      total number of comparisons made during the sort

    • getSwaps

      public long getSwaps()
      Returns:
      total number of swaps made during the sort

    • getAccesses

      public long getAccesses()
      Returns:
      total number of array accesses during the sort

    • getAlgorithmName

      public String getAlgorithmName()
      Returns:
      the name of the sorting algorithm

    • getGeneratorLabel

      public String getGeneratorLabel()
      Returns:
      the label describing what kind of input was used

    • getElapsedMs

      public long getElapsedMs()
      Returns how long the sort took in milliseconds. If the sort is still running, it returns the time elapsed so far. Returns 0 if start() was never called.
      Returns:
      elapsed time in ms

    • isFinished

      public boolean isFinished()
      Checks whether the sort has finished.
      Returns:
      true if the sort is done, false if it's still running

    • getGeneratorType

      public String getGeneratorType()
      Returns:
      the type of generator that produced the input list

    • getSize

      public int getSize()
      Returns:
      the size of the list that was sorted, or -1 if not set

    • getEntropy

      public double getEntropy()
      Returns:
      the entropy of the input (0.0 = fully sorted, 1.0 = fully random), or -1 if not set

    • getRun

      public int getRun()
      Returns:
      which run number this was in the benchmark, or -1 if not set
